title: Breaking Down the Israel–Iran Conflict: What We Know So Far - Day 5
byline: This guide answers the most important questions about the Israel Iran war that is unfolding right now. The United States and Israel have launched major military strikes on Iran and Iran has responded with missile attacks across the region. This is one of the most serious escalations in the Middle East in years.

title: ICC Men’s T20 World Cup 2026: From Super 8s to Semi-Finals – Full Update
byline: Follow the latest ICC Men’s T20 World Cup 2026 updates as the tournament reaches the semi-final stage. See which four teams have qualified, how they advanced from the Super 8s, and what lies ahead in the race to the final.

title: Allen’s 33-ball century sends New Zealand storming into T20 World Cup Final
byline: Finn Allen smashed the fastest century in T20 World Cup history as New Zealand national cricket team crushed the South Africa national cricket team by nine wickets in their semi-final at Eden Gardens.

title: Allen’s 33-ball century rewrites T20 World Cup history
byline: Finn Allen delivered one of the most explosive knocks ever seen in a T20 World Cup, rocketing from a 19-ball fifty to a breathtaking 33-ball hundred.

title: Allen’s record ton rockets New Zealand through
byline: Finn Allen smashed the fastest century in T20 World Cup history as New Zealand national cricket team crushed the South Africa national cricket team by nine wickets in their semi-final at Eden Gardens.

title: Middle East war spills over to Indian Ocean, adjacent to Sri Lanka
byline: Indian Ocean security appears to be in jeopardy after an Iranian warship sank just outside Sri Lanka’s territorial waters in the midst of war flaring up in the Middle East following the United States and Israel attack on Iran, which launched retaliatory strikes.

title: PUSCL confirms possible power cuts as a result of inferior quality coal
byline: The report of the Public Utilities Commission has confirmed that there is a risk of power cuts as a result of inferior coal, it was reported yesterday.

title: US Senate fails to advance war powers measure to rein in Trump’s Iran war
byline: The United States Senate has failed to pass a resolution seeking to rein in US President Donald Trump’s war with Iran.

title: US strikes Iranian warship within Sri Lanka's Exclusive Economic Zone
byline: In the midst of the Middle East war escalating, over 100 people are missing, and dozens of others have been wounded after an Iranian warship, IRIS Dena- a Moudge-class frigate from the Southern Fleet of Iran Navy- sank within the Exclusive Economic Zone of Sri Lanka following a torpedo strike by a U.S. submarine.

title: India slams ’baseless’ claim of US using its ports
byline: The widening US-Iran conflict has now reached the Indian Ocean, but India has made it clear that it will not be pulled into the war narrative.

title: Attack on Iranian ship within Sri Lanka’s Exclusive Economic Zone a death blow to Sri Lanka’s sovereignty: Sajith
byline: Opposition Leader Sajith Premadasa said today that military action cannot be carried out within an Exclusive Economic Zone (EEZ) of a country according to international laws and that the attack on the Iranian warship within Sri Lanka’s Exclusive Economic Zone is a death blow to Sri Lanka’s sovereignty.

title: Netanyahu ‘fears US and Iran holding secret talks’
byline: Benjamin Netanyahu is reportedly concerned the US is holding secret talks with Iran behind his back.

title: Customs seize Rs. 536m hashish, ICE in air cargo
byline: Lanka Customs officers attached to the Laksiri Seva (UPB) Warehouse have intercepted a major consignment of illegal narcotics concealed in food packages during the inspection of air cargo shipments from Malaysia.

title: Another Iranian vessel in Sri Lanka’s Exclusive Economic Zone: Nalinda
byline: Chief Government Whip, Minister Nalinda Jayatissa said today there is another Iranian vessel, Iranian Naval Ship (IRINS) Bushehr, within Sri Lanka’s Exclusive Economic Zone (EEZ).

title: 10-year concessionary loan scheme at 5% for small businesses in Nuwara Eliya
byline: The Ministry of Industries has initiated a new loan scheme to support the development of small businesses in the Nuwara Eliya District, offering loans at a concessionary interest rate of five per cent to be repaid over a period of 10 years.

title: Qatar Airways to start limited relief flights for stranded passengers from Muscat, Riyadh beginning today
byline: Qatar Airways will keep its flight operations remain temporarily suspended due to the continued closure of Qatari airspace, with services set to resume only after authorities declare it safe to reopen. However, the airlines said to operate a limited number of relief flights from today.

title: Many things happening within Indian Ocean than you think: Govt tells opposition
byline: The Sri Lankan government is working towards maintaining peace within the Indian ocean, Chief Government Whip Nalinda Jayatissa told Parliament today.

title: Retired Health Ministry chief clerk arrested over Rs. 20 million bribe
byline: A retired Chief Clerk of the Ministry of Health has been arrested for allegedly demanding and accepting a bribe of Rs. 20 million.

title: Govt in talks with ICC to hand Sri Lanka Cricket to Interim Committee: Minister
byline: Minister of Youth Affairs and Sports, Mr. Sunil Kumara Gamage said that discussions are underway with the International Cricket Council (ICC) to hand over the administration of Sri Lankan cricket to an Interim Committee aimed at rebuilding the sport.

title: Experts warn of health risks to children as air quality turns unhealthy
byline: With air pollution levels in Colombo rising to unhealthy levels, health experts are raising alarm over the potential impact on public health, particularly on children.

title: Customs foil major cannabis smuggling attempt at BIA
byline: Officers attached to the Narcotics Control Unit of Sri Lanka Customs intercepted two Indian nationals attempting to smuggle a large consignment of narcotics into the country via Bandaranaike International Airport (BIA) yeaterday.

title: Iran’s internet plummets to 1% as blackout enters fifth day
byline: ’’Iran’s internet blackout has now exceeded 120 hours with connectivity still flatlining around 1% of ordinary levels,’’ Netblocks said in a message posted on social media platform X.

title: Wimal questions Govt. over alleged U.S. influence regarding Iranian vessel
byline: Former Member of Parliament and National Freedom Front (NFF) leader Wimal Weerawansa has raised serious concerns regarding the Sri Lankan government’s handling of an Iranian naval vessel currently stationed near the island’s territorial waters.

title: Sri Lanka deliberates on Iranian vessel near territorial waters
byline: Sri Lankan authorities are currently discussing the steps to be taken regarding another Iranian vessel lingering near the country’s territorial waters, a source said.

title: School students educated on dangers of narcotics
byline: School students were educated on the dangers of narcotic drugs as part of a series of awareness programmes being carried out under the National Mission for the Nation Together initiative, the Public Administration, Provincial Councils and Local Government Ministry said.

title: Fishermen requested not to go beyond Sri Lankan territorial waters
byline: Fisheries Minister Ramalingam Chandrasekar today requested Sri Lankan fishmen not to intrude into International waters violating Sri Lanka’s territorial waters.

title: Bodies of Iranian sailors kept on ice as mortuary exceeds capacity
byline: Several bodies of Iranian sailors killed in the sinking of the warship IRIS Dena are being kept on ice outside the Karapitiya Hospital mortuary after the facility exceeded its storage capacity, hospital sources said.

title: Middle East instability threatens over half of Sri Lanka’s tea exports: Planters’ Association
byline: The ongoing military instability in the Middle East could place more than half of Sri Lanka’s tea exports at risk, Chairman of the National Tea Planters Association Saman Geeganage has warned.

title: SriLankan Airlines to operate extra Colombo–London flight on March 8
byline: SriLankan Airlines will operate an additional flight between Colombo and London on March 8, 2026 to assist passengers affected by flight cancellations caused by the ongoing situation in the Middle East.

title: Iran requests return of all bodies from IRIS Dena to Tehran
byline: The Iranian authorities have requested Sri Lanka to hand over all the bodies of the sailors who died in the attack onboard the IRIS Dena so they can be repatriated for final rites.

title: Jamie Siddons appointed Head Coach of Sri Lanka Women’s Cricket team
byline: Sri Lanka Cricket has appointed former Australian cricketer Jamie Siddons as the Head Coach of the Sri Lanka National Women’s Team.

title: Communist Party of Sri  Lanka condemns attack on Iranian warship off southern coast
byline: The Communist Party of Sri Lanka condemned the illegal sinking of the Iranian frigate IRIS Dena by the US Navy within the Exclusive Economic Zone (EEZ) of Sri Lanka.

title: ‘IRIS Dena was Indian Navy guest, hit without warning’: Iran warns US of bitter regret
byline: A day after a US submarine sunk an Iranian Navy warship off the coast of Sri Lanka, the Foreign Minister of Iran, Sayed Abbas Araghchi, has warned that the US would ’’pay bitterly’’ for targeting a ship in international waters.

title: CEB denies employees’ involvement in alleged assault
byline: The Ceylon Electricity Board (CEB) has denied reports circulating in certain media outlets claiming that a group of its employees assaulted an individual.

title: Power cuts reported in multiple areas
byline: A technical fault in the electricity supply system between Anuradhapura and Vavuniya triggered power cuts in multiple areas across the island, the Ceylon Electricity Board (CEB) said.

title: Fuel shortage threatens paddy harvest of 800,000 acres – Farmers’ Union
byline: The Chairman of the National Farmers’ Union, Anuradha Tennakoon, has warned that this year’s harvesting operations are at serious risk as the government reportedly has fuel stocks sufficient for only one month, while at least eight million litres of diesel are required to harvest around 800,000 acres of paddy fields.

title: Namal urges govt to clarify stance on Iranian vessel seeking port call
byline: National Organiser of the Sri Lanka Podujana Peramuna (SLPP) Namal Rajapaksa has called on the government to immediately clarify its position regarding another Iranian vessel that is reportedly within Sri Lanka’s Exclusive Economic Zone (EEZ) and seeking permission to make an urgent port call.

title: India slams ’baseless’ claim of US using its ports
byline: The widening US-Iran conflict has now reached the Indian Ocean, but India has made it clear that it will not be pulled into the war narrative.

title: Netanyahu ‘fears US and Iran holding secret talks’
byline: Benjamin Netanyahu is reportedly concerned the US is holding secret talks with Iran behind his back.

title: Nepalis vote in first election since Gen Z uprising for change
byline: Millions of Nepalis have voted in the country’s first general election since violent youth-led protests toppled the government last September.

title: Market Extends Rally as Strong Volumes Signal Renewed Confidence
byline: The Colombo market continued its upward trajectory on 05th March 2026, building on the previous session’s recovery with stronger momentum. Improved turnover and a significant increase in share volumes underscored strengthening investor sentiment. The presence of sizeable block trades further signaled growing confidence among value investors, offering a constructive outlook for near-term market stability.

title: US-Israeli Strike Destroys 12,000-Seat Azadi Stadium Hall in Tehran
byline: A 12,000-seat indoor arena within Tehran’s Azadi Sports Complex was destroyed in a US-Israeli airstrike today, marking the latest in a series of criminal attacks targeting Iranian infrastructure.

title: Female attorney jailed over forged Borella land deeds
byline: A female lawyer who was found guilty of preparing forged deeds was today sentenced to six years’ imprisonment by Colombo High Court Judge Lanka Jayaratne.

title: Millions of Cubans plunged into darkness as fuel crisis deepens
byline: Millions of people were left without power in Cuba on Wednesday - the latest in a string of blackouts to hit the island as it struggles with fuel shortages worsened by pressure from the US.

title: Vijitha Herath telephones Iranian Foreign Minister; expresses grave concern over  escalation of hostilities
byline: Foreign Miniter Vijitha Herath said he telephoned Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi on the rapidly evolving situation in the Middle East.

title: Met Dept issues heat advisory
byline: The Department of Meteorology has issued a warm weather advisory for March 6, 2026, highlighting potential health risks due to elevated heat conditions. The warning specifically targets Sabaragamuwa province and the districts of Kurunegala, Gampaha, and Monaragala, where the heat index is expected to reach a “Caution” level.

title: New explosions heard over Qatar, Bahrain
byline: New explosions have been heard over Qatar and Bahrain amid Iranian missile strikes across the region, News.Az reports, citing Al Jazeera.

title: Iranian drones hit airport in Azerbaijan’s exclave as US-Israel war widens
byline: An Iranian drone attack has targeted Azerbaijan’s autonomous Nakhchivan exclave, opening yet another front in the ongoing war launched by the United States and Israel and the retaliation from Tehran.

title: How is Sri Lanka responding to the Israel–Iran conflict: Day 6
byline: As tensions escalate between Israel and Iran, Sri Lanka has taken a series of diplomatic, aviation and precautionary measures to manage potential fallout and safeguard its citizens. Here is how Sri Lanka is responding.

title: Iranian vessel crew to be disembarked at Colombo Port, ship to be moved to Trincomalee: President
byline: Sri Lanka will disembark the crew of an Iranian vessel currently near its territorial waters and move the ship to Trincomalee Harbour, President Anura Kumara Dissanayake said today.

title: India beat England in thriller, March into Final
byline: India edged England by seven runs in a dramatic T20 World Cup semi-final in Mumbai on Thursday, surviving a breathtaking century from Jacob Bethell to book their place in the final.

title: Iranian crew to be brought to Colombo
byline: Sri Lankan President Anura Kumara Dissanayake said that the crew of the Iranian naval ship IRIS Bushehr, currently positioned just outside Sri Lanka’s territorial waters, will be disembarked at the Port of Colombo.

title: Fifth national oral health survey to begin on March 10
byline: The Fifth National Oral Health Survey will commence on March 10, aimed at assessing the oral health status of people across the country.
